description: Use 3C/3D as artificial raises after 1M to show different strength levels
purpose: Distinguish between weak, constructive, limit, and game-forcing raises of partner's major so opener can judge the right level
scopeNote: Encodes the originally published Bergen Raises over a 1-of-major opening: `3C` = 6-10 with 4+ support, `3D` = 11-12 limit raise with 4+ support, direct `3M` raise = 0-5 with 4+ support, plus the `3NT` = 13-15 balanced 4-3-3-3 add-on Larry recommends. The 'inversion revision' (swapping 3C / 3D), passed-hand usage, competitive usage, and 4-level splinter variants are out of scope; Larry explicitly recommends not using Bergen by a passed hand or after double/overcall. 3-of-the-other-major is authored here as a splinter (short suit, game+ values), a partnership-style extension not explicitly pinned by the Larry Cohen authority, retained as a default so the 3-level is fully defined. Opener rebids and responder follow-up continuations after each raise tier are authored against BridgeBum's Bergen reference (cited as `references.discovery`), not the Larry Cohen authority snapshot; Jacoby 2NT is a separate module.

authority.label: Larry Cohen — co-inventor of Bergen Raises, authoritative on standard vs reverse variants

snapshot.text:
# Bergen Raises

By: Larry Cohen

## BERGEN RAISES

This convention was first invented by my good friend, Marty Bergen, when we were bridge partners in the 1980's.

As first written and played, here were the responses to a 1-of-a-Major opening:

3♣ = 6-10 in support and 4-card support

3♦ = 11-12 in support (limit raise) and 4-card support

3-of-the-major (raise) = 0-5 and 4-card support

There are many add-ons and revisions, some as follows:

Inversion revision (make sure you and your partner know if you are playing the original way or this way):

The 3♣ and 3♦ responses are reversed in meaning

## Major Add-ons

A jump to 3-of-the other major has a special meaning (there are many ways out there--I suggest not bidding it)

3NT = 13-15 balanced with 4-3-3-3 (I recommend this)

4-level = Many possibilities (In "Standard" it would just be a An unusual jump showing 0-1 in that suit and support for partner Splinter Bid).

## PH and Competition

It is crucial to have agreements regarding if this convention is used by a Passed Hand (I recommend not) or after a double or overcall (I recommend not). Some variations use the convention in all of these situations.

Because of all the potential confusion (all 3 of the sections above), I don't recommend this convention (sorry, Marty). I actually like a jump to 3-of-a-lower suit to be natural and invitation. So, 1♠-3♦ shows something like:

♠2
♥32
♦AQJ10876
♣K32

You must decide with your partner if you are playing Bergen Raises, how you are playing them, or a possible alternative.

teaching.tradeoff: Uses 3C and 3D as conventional raises, giving up natural minor bids over 1M.
teaching.principle: Immediately show trump support and strength — the Law of Total Tricks says bid to the level of your combined trump length.

commonMistakes.text: Do not treat Bergen as a three-card raise structure.
commonMistakes.reason: Bergen promises four-card support. With only three-card limit support, route through 1NT forcing and then a 3M rebid instead.

commonMistakes.text: Do not confuse the constructive and limit steps.
commonMistakes.reason: Reversing 3♣ and 3♦ gives opener the wrong picture of responder's strength and game odds.

commonMistakes.text: Do not bury a game-forcing shortage hand inside Bergen.
commonMistakes.reason: Shortage plus strong support belongs in a splinter so opener can evaluate slam and wasted values correctly.

explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: HCP outside the system's 1NT response range

explanationEntries.displayText: Opening 1♥: 12+ HCP with 5+ hearts
Opening 1♠: 12+ HCP with 5+ spades
Splinter in hearts: 12+ pts, 4+ trumps, shortness
Bergen limit raise in hearts: 11-12 pts, 4+ support (3♦)
Bergen constructive raise in hearts: 6-10 pts, 4+ support (3♣)
Preemptive raise to 3♥: 0-5 pts, 4+ support
Natural 1NT response to 1♥: within system range, no 4+ support
Direct 3NT over 1♥: 13-15 HCP, flat 4-3-3-3, no 4+ major support
Splinter in spades: 12+ pts, 4+ trumps, shortness
Bergen limit raise in spades: 11-12 pts, 4+ support (3♦)
Bergen constructive raise in spades: 6-10 pts, 4+ support (3♣)
Preemptive raise to 3♠: 0-5 pts, 4+ support
Natural 1NT response to 1♠: within system range, no 4+ support
Direct 3NT over 1♠: 13-15 HCP, flat 4-3-3-3, no 4+ major support
Opener bids 4♥ after constructive raise (17+ HCP)
Opener makes a game try with 3♦ after constructive raise (14-16 HCP)
Opener bids 3♠ as a natural spade slam try after the constructive heart raise
Opener signs off in 3♥ after constructive raise
Opener bids 4♠ after constructive raise (17+ HCP)
Opener makes a game try with 3♥ after constructive raise (14-16 HCP)
Opener signs off in 3♠ after constructive raise
Opener accepts limit raise and bids 4♥ (15+ HCP)
Opener declines limit raise and signs off in 3♥
Opener bids 3♠ as a natural spade slam try after the limit heart raise
Opener accepts limit raise and bids 4♠ (15+ HCP)
Opener makes a help-suit game try with 3♥ after the limit spade raise
Opener declines limit raise and signs off in 3♠
Opener bids 4♥ despite preemptive raise (18+ total pts)
Opener passes after preemptive raise to 3♥
Opener bids 4♠ despite preemptive raise (18+ total pts)
Opener passes after preemptive raise to 3♠
Accept game try and bid 4♥
Accept game try and bid 4♠
Reject game try and sign off in 3♥
Reject game try and sign off in 3♠
Responder accepts opener's game bid
Responder accepts opener's signoff
Opener accepts game after responder accepts the try
Opener's 3NT relay asks responder to reveal the short suit
Opener's 3♠ relay asks responder to reveal the short suit
4♣: shortness in clubs
4♦: shortness in diamonds
4♥: shortness in the other major (spades)
3NT: shortness in clubs
4♣: shortness in diamonds
4♦: shortness in the other major (hearts)
Opener signs off in 4♥ with a minimum — no slam interest after the splinter
Opener signs off in 4♠ with a minimum — no slam interest after the splinter

scope.reason: BridgeBum treats opener's 3♠ rebid after a heart raise as a natural slam try. Responder's continuation is partnership judgment rather than a fixed Bergen table, so this fixture stops at opener's descriptive call.
BridgeBum gives 1♠-3♦-3♥ as a help-suit game try. Responder's follow-up depends on fit and honor evaluation, so this fixture stops at opener's descriptive call.

summaryCard.trigger: Partner opens one major and responder already knows there is support.
summaryCard.partnership: Bergen pairs three clubs and three diamonds as peer entry points that both promise four-card-or-better major support; the conventional act is choosing between them, with three clubs as the constructive raise and three diamonds as the limit raise.

whenToUse.gloss: Use Bergen's obstructive raises with weak support hands.
Use Bergen to separate constructive and limit raises with support.
Use the splinter branch with strong support and shortness.

workedAuctions.label: Constructive raise accepts a help-suit try

calls.rationale: Responder uses the constructive Bergen raise.
Opener makes a help-suit game try.
Responder accepts with the top of the constructive range.

workedAuctions.label: Limit raise lets opener judge game immediately

calls.rationale: Responder shows the limit Bergen raise.
Opener accepts the invitation with enough values for game.

workedAuctions.label: Strong support with shortness becomes a splinter sequence

calls.rationale: Responder uses the spade splinter, showing strong support with shortness.
Opener relays to ask where the shortage is.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: three-card support overstates the trump fit

calls.rationale: Responder uses Bergen anyway, even though the hand has only three-card support.

items.ourAction: Many partnerships switch to Jordan or redouble structure instead of plain Bergen.
items.note: The double changes both the available room and the standard meanings of jump raises.

items.ourAction: Bergen is usually off and the raise structure becomes cue-bids, fit jumps, or natural competition.
items.note: Once opponents enter, the standard step map no longer has enough uncontested room.

items.recommendation: Simple raise in the major, or use the preemptive jump raise when your partnership's Bergen style applies.
items.note: Downgraded view: this list cannot separate the ordinary single raise from the jump raise cleanly.
Constructive and limit raises share this bucket in the downgraded view, because the missing trump-support fact prevents a full Bergen grid.
This fixture keeps the ambiguous splinter branch; flat thirteen-to-fifteen hands without four-card-or-better major support use Larry's three-notrump add-on.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Bergen shows support immediately after one major; NMF checks back later after opener limits the hand with one notrump.
Both conventions aim at major-suit games, but Bergen starts from an agreed fit while Stayman asks whether a fit exists.

teaching.purpose: Show the right level of support when partner opens 1 of a major and you have a 4+ fit
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1H or 1S and you have 4+ cards in their major, or you have the 13-15 flat 4-3-3-3 hand for Larry's direct 3NT add-on. Choose between the preemptive raise (0-5), constructive raise (6-10), limit raise (11-12), and splinter branch (12+ with shortage).
whenNotToUse: Fewer than 4 cards in partner's major and not the 13-15 flat 4-3-3-3 add-on — look for other bids
Passed hand — Bergen raises are off after a passed hand
After opponent interference — standard raises apply instead
teaching.tradeoff: 3C and 3D lose their natural minor-suit meanings in favor of showing major support
teaching.principle: With a known trump fit, preempt with weakness, separate constructive and limit support precisely, and use the splinter branch when shortness changes opener's evaluation
teaching.roles: Opener evaluates whether the partnership has game after responder's Bergen bid

definitions.description: Has 4+ support in at least one major
Flat 4-3-3-3 shape with no 4+ major support
